paper_title: Taxonomic and Quantitative Analysis of Foraminifera

methods.description: This method involves the taxonomic classification and analysis of microfossils, particularly foraminifera, to understand their preservation state and assign them to specific taxa or morphogroups.

steps.description: Assess the state of preservation of microfossils in the samples.
Assign microfossils to morphogroups or taxa based on their preservation state and morphological features.
steps.searchable_summary: classify microfossils into morphogroups or taxa

steps.description: Merge certain taxa based on observed morphological transitions.
steps.searchable_summary: merge taxa based on morphological transitions

methods.description: This method describes the process of collecting and preparing samples for quantitative analysis of foraminifera.

steps.description: Sample the Radotruncana calcarata Taxon Range Zone bed-by-bed.
steps.searchable_summary: collect samples from specific stratigraphic zones

steps.description: Soak samples in tenside Rewoquad c for 24 hours and rinse with water.
steps.searchable_summary: soak and rinse samples with tenside

steps.description: Soak samples in hydrogen peroxide (35%) for 1 hour and wet sieve.
steps.searchable_summary: treat samples with hydrogen peroxide and sieve

steps.description: Cook firm foraminifera packstone samples in hydrogen peroxide for ten minutes and use tensides repeatedly.
steps.searchable_summary: intensively treat firm samples with hydrogen peroxide and tensides

steps.description: Wash disaggregated samples over 63 µm and 125 µm mesh sieves and dry overnight at 60°C.

methods.description: This method involves the quantitative analysis of foraminiferal assemblages to determine species composition and abundance.

steps.description: Analyze the composition of foraminiferal community in the R. calcarata TRZ.
Record the number of specimens and relative abundances of foraminiferal species.
steps.searchable_summary: record species abundance and specimen count

steps.description: Use PanPlot 2 to visualize foraminifera abundances in the Postalm section.
